patterns.label: Liferay OSGi Unresolved Constraint
patterns.regex: Org\.osgi\.framework\.BundleException: Could not resolve module:*|Unresolved constraint in bundle.*
patterns.summary: An OSGi bundle failed to start or resolve due to missing dependencies or version mismatches.

suggestions.title: Check Gogo Shell Diagnostic
suggestions.description: The OSGi container is missing a required package or bundle.
manual_steps: Identify the missing package from the error message (e.g. 'Import-Package: com.liferay.portal.kernel').
Verify the version range in `bnd.bnd` matches the versions available in the portal.
Run `diag <bundle-id>` in the Liferay Gogo shell to see specific missing requirements.

patterns.label: Blade Deploy Failure
patterns.regex: Error: blade deploy failed|blade server start failed
patterns.summary: Liferay Blade CLI failed to deploy a bundle or manage the server process.

suggestions.title: Clean and Deploy
suggestions.description: The deployment environment might be in an inconsistent state.
manual_steps: Verify the Liferay server is running and accessible.
Run `./gradlew clean deploy` or `blade deploy` again.
Check for file path issues in `blade.properties`.

patterns.label: Java Class/Method Not Found
patterns.regex: java\.lang\.ClassNotFoundException|java\.lang\.NoClassDefFoundError|java\.lang\.NoSuchMethodError
patterns.summary: A fatal Java runtime error indicating that a class or method present at compile time is missing at runtime (Dependency Conflict).

suggestions.title: Audit Dependency Tree
suggestions.description: Multiple versions of the same library might be present in the classpath.
manual_steps: Identify the library providing the missing class.
Run `mvn dependency:tree` or `./gradlew dependencies`.
Exclude conflicting transitive dependencies in `pom.xml` or `build.gradle`.

patterns.label: Spring Bean Creation Failure
patterns.regex: org\.springframework\.beans\.factory\.BeanCreationException|Error creating bean with name
patterns.summary: Spring Boot failed to initialize one or more beans, often due to missing configuration or circular dependencies.

suggestions.title: Verify Configuration Properties
suggestions.description: Check if all required `@Value` or `@ConfigurationProperties` are provided.
manual_steps: Look for 'Unsatisfied dependency expressed' in the logs to find the root cause.
Ensure the database or external service is reachable if the bean is a DAO/Repository.
Check `application.properties` or `application.yml` for missing keys.

patterns.label: ESLint Quality Failure
patterns.regex: \d+ errors found \(\d+ errors potentially fixable with the `--fix` option\)
patterns.summary: The build was blocked by linting violations.

suggestions.title: Run Lint Fix
suggestions.description: Some or all errors can be automatically corrected by ESLint.
manual_steps: Run `npm run lint -- --fix` or `yarn lint --fix` locally.
Review any remaining manual errors and fix them following the project's style guide.

patterns.label: React Hydration Error
patterns.regex: Hydration failed because the initial UI does not match what was rendered on the server
patterns.summary: In Next.js or SSR React applications, the client-side render differs from the server-side HTML.

suggestions.title: Identify Non-Deterministic Logic
suggestions.description: Often caused by using `window`, `localStorage`, or random numbers during the initial render.
manual_steps: Ensure components use `useEffect` for client-only logic.
Check for mismatched HTML tags (e.g. `p` inside `p`).
Use a tool like `react-hydration-on-demand` to debug specific components.

patterns.label: Vite/Webpacker Compilation Error
patterns.regex: error within .* during Vite .* build|failed to compile with \d+ errors
patterns.summary: Frontend assets failed to compile, likely due to syntax errors or missing modules.

suggestions.title: Clear Build Cache
suggestions.description: Corruption in the `.vite` or `node_modules/.cache` directory can cause persistent failures.
manual_steps: Delete the `node_modules` and `dist`/`build` folders.
Run `npm install` and `npm run build` again.
Check for broken imports or missing `.jsx`/`.tsx` extensions.

patterns.label: Python Import/Module Error
patterns.regex: ImportError: No module named .*|ModuleNotFoundError: No module named .*
patterns.summary: Python was unable to import a required module, usually because it's not installed in the virtual environment.

suggestions.title: Update Requirements
suggestions.description: Ensure `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml` is up to date and all packages are installed.
manual_steps: Verify the package is in `requirements.txt`.
Run `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
Check for typos in the import statement or module name.

patterns.label: Django Migration Conflict
patterns.regex: django\.db\.utils\.OperationalError: .*|django\.db\.migrations\.exceptions\.InconsistentMigrationHistory
patterns.summary: Django failed to apply database migrations, often due to a schema mismatch or inconsistent migration state.

suggestions.title: Inspect Migration State
suggestions.description: The database schema might be out of sync with the codebase.
manual_steps: Run `python manage.py makemigrations --merge` if there are conflicting branches.
Run `python manage.py migrate --show-status` to see which migrations have been applied.
As a last resort, manually resolve the state in the `django_migrations` table.

patterns.label: FastAPI/Pydantic Validation Error
patterns.regex: pydantic_core\._pydantic_core\.ValidationError: \d+ validation errors for .*
patterns.summary: FastAPI failed to start or validate a request because the data did not match the Pydantic schema.

suggestions.title: Check Environment Variables or Request Body
suggestions.description: Commonly occurs during startup if environment variables are missing.
manual_steps: Review the list of missing fields in the validation error.
Ensure the `.env` file or environment has all required keys.
Check if the request payload matches the model definition in `schemas.py`.

patterns.label: Java Compilation Failure
patterns.regex: ^.+\.java:\d+: error: .+
patterns.summary: One or more Java source files failed to compile. Common causes include syntax errors, missing imports, or incompatible API changes.

suggestions.title: Check compiler error lines in the log
suggestions.description: Identify the specific file and line number from the error message and open the source file to inspect the issue.
manual_steps: Search the log for lines matching the compiler error pattern.
Open the indicated source file at the reported line number.
Correct the syntax error, missing import, or type mismatch.

patterns.label: Maven Dependency Resolution Failure
patterns.regex: \[ERROR\] Failed to execute goal .* on project .*: Could not resolve dependencies
patterns.summary: Maven was unable to resolve one or more project dependencies.

suggestions.title: Verify dependency coordinates
suggestions.description: Ensure the artifact exists and the network can reach the repository.
manual_steps: Check `pom.xml` for typos.
Run `mvn clean install -U` locally.
Check for proxy/firewall issues.

patterns.label: Gradle Build Failure
patterns.regex: > Task .* FAILED
patterns.summary: A Gradle task failed to execute correctly.

suggestions.title: Inspect task output
suggestions.description: Review the specific failure message associated with the failed Gradle task.
manual_steps: Search the log for the specific task name that failed.
Run `./gradlew <task-name> --stacktrace` locally to get more detail.

patterns.label: npm/yarn Dependency Error
patterns.regex: npm ERR! .+|error Combined compiled stacktrace|yarn error .+
patterns.summary: A Node.js dependency installation or build step failed.

suggestions.title: Clear node_modules and retry
suggestions.description: Often caused by inconsistent state or registry connectivity issues.
manual_steps: Verify `package.json` syntax.
Run `npm cache clean --force` and retry.
Check for lockfile conflicts.

patterns.label: Docker Build Failure
patterns.regex: The command '/bin/sh -c .*' returned a non-zero code: \d+
patterns.summary: A command inside the Dockerfile failed to execute.

suggestions.title: Debug Dockerfile layer
suggestions.description: The specific instruction in the Dockerfile is failing.
manual_steps: Identify which RUN command failed.
Try to run the base image locally and execute the command interactively.

patterns.label: Out of Memory (OOM) Kill
patterns.regex: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError|Killed|exit code 137
patterns.summary: The process was terminated because it exceeded memory limits or the JVM ran out of heap space.

suggestions.title: Increase Resource Limits
suggestions.description: Allocate more memory to the Jenkins agent or the JVM process.
manual_steps: Increase `-Xmx` heap size for JVM processes.
Adjust container memory limits in the Jenkinsfile/Pod definition.

patterns.label: Build/Task Timeout
patterns.regex: Aborted by .*|Terminated after \d+ ms|Build timed out
patterns.summary: The build or a specific stage exceeded the configured timeout limit.

suggestions.title: Investigate Hanging Processes
suggestions.description: Identify if a process is hanging or if the timeout limit is too restrictive.
manual_steps: Check for infinite loops in tests.
Identify slow network operations.
Increase the timeout limit in the pipeline definition if necessary.

patterns.label: Permission Denied / Access Denied
patterns.regex: Permission denied|Access is denied|EACCES
patterns.summary: The Jenkins process lacks necessary permissions to read/write a file or execute a command.

suggestions.title: Check File/Directory Permissions
suggestions.description: Verify the Jenkins user has the correct UID/GID permissions.
manual_steps: Check `ls -l` on the affected path.
Ensure the Jenkins agent is running as the expected user.

patterns.label: No Space Left on Device
patterns.regex: No space left on device|Disk full|ENOSPC
patterns.summary: The Jenkins agent or the underlying host has run out of disk space.

suggestions.title: Clean Workspace and Temporary Files
suggestions.description: Free up space on the agent or scale the disk.
manual_steps: Wipe out the Jenkins workspace.
Check `/tmp` and `/var/log` for large files.
Prune unused Docker images/volumes: `docker system prune`.

patterns.label: Automated Test Failure
patterns.regex: Tests run: \d+, Failures: [1-9]\d*|FAILED \(failures=\d+\)|[1-9]\d* failed, .* passed
patterns.summary: One or more automated tests failed.

suggestions.title: Inspect Test Reports
suggestions.description: Review the test results to identify the failing test cases.
manual_steps: Check JUnit/TestNG/pytest reports.
Look for stacktraces in the console output below the failure summary.
